personality_voice.book_baseline: Sirius Black does not appear in the book corpus provided. personality_voice.penalty_logic: No score can be assigned. The corpus contains no material depicting Sirius Black as a character in either medium.

lost_or_transferred_material: The book corpus contains a single mention of Sirius Black by Hagrid ('Young Sirius Black lent it to me') establishing his existence and his connection to Hagrid and the motorcycle. This line is absent from the film corpus, where the motorcycle's owner is not named.
key_observations: The provided corpus — both book and film — covers only Philosopher's Stone material, in which Sirius Black is not a present character. He is named once in the book corpus (Hagrid's line about the motorcycle) and not at all in the film corpus. There is no basis on which to evaluate any dimension of his portrayal. All scores are 0 and all confidence levels are Low, reflecting the complete absence of evaluable material, not a judgment that the films portray him unfaithfully. A meaningful FP evaluation of Sirius Black would require corpus material from Prisoner of Azkaban onward.
